_meta.source_series: S2 — Building the Agentic Harness (core mechanics) + S1 B10-12 (chapter walkthroughs)
_meta.note: Mechanics RENDER cited S2 passages; walkthroughs are the /seeit/<book>-chN pages S1 B10-12 link to. Book↔code: sourced + hashed; re-run to auto-refresh.

topics.title: B32 Obligation Packets
topics.operator_explanation: Every commitment is a receipt with two sides: opening it is a DRAFT (nothing happens until you approve), closing it requires EVIDENCE. So every piece of work is tracked, provable, and reversible — you can always see what's open, what's done, and the proof behind it.

source.book: S2 Vol 3 — The Governed Dev Loop
source.passage: **Reader-effort calibration:** Reading time ~90 minutes for the manuscript proper. Hands-on time with the `sovereign-agent-starter` demos ~3 hours (six core demos × ~30 minutes each, including JSON-receipt inspection + role spec editing + cross-role veto reproduction). The deep-dive companion's specialist depth assumes the reader will engage at both layers; the volume's claims are only fully redeemable through hands-on engagement.

topics.title: K1–K4 Constitutional Invariants
topics.operator_explanation: Four rules the system ALWAYS enforces, no exceptions. The first and most important — K1, human primacy — means you stay in command: agents propose, you dispose. Nothing material happens without your breath. The others guarantee receipts, fail-closed safety, and honest errors.

source.passage: - **Volume 1 — *Sovereign Inference & Memory*:** the receipted inference foundation. SIX-lane routing, B51 human-memory streaming, receipts as constitutional anchors. The trust layer everything else rests on. - **Volume 2 (this book) — *The Primacy Cockpit*:** the surface where human primacy is visible. The Atrium — every screen built so the operator can see what the agents are doing, what the constitution is enforcing, what's waiting for human breath, and what has already been sealed. - **Volume 3 — *The Harness That Builds Itself — Under Human Witness*:** the governed dev loop. Operator-defined roles, proposal mechanics, the hard constitutional boundary, the drift safeguard at depth.

topics.title: Merkle Anchors (Receipted Memory)
topics.operator_explanation: Everything the system does is written to a tamper-evident chain — each entry locked to the one before it by a cryptographic hash. Like a sealed ledger: if anything were altered, the chain breaks visibly. It's how you can PROVE what happened and when, years later.

source.section: Build the Receipted Cockpit That Keeps Humans in Command
source.passage: **Sovereign Inference & Memory: Build the Receipted Cockpit That Keeps Humans in Command**

topics.title: The Governed Loop
topics.operator_explanation: How a thought becomes a sealed change: capture → packet → validate → ACCEPT (your one gate) → apply → seal. The agents do the work in the background and bring you a single decision; nothing lands without your Accept. One human gate, everything else automated.

source.book: S2 Vol 3 — The Governed Dev Loop
source.passage: This is **Volume 3** of *Building the Agentic Harness* — the **deep-dive companion** volume. Where Vols 1-2 + 4-5 deliver broader-audience treatments (operator-builder + family-office + general technical-architect audiences), Vol 3 deep-dives the *governed dev loop* for the specialist audience: senior developers, platform architects, and constitutional-governance specialists responsible for configuring, governing, and extending agentic systems in production where drift is a board-level risk.

topics.title: Atrium Surfaces (The Cockpit)
topics.operator_explanation: The Atrium is your cockpit — one place to see everything: what the agents are doing, what the constitution is enforcing, what's waiting for your breath, and what's already sealed. You never leave it; every part of the system surfaces here so you stay sovereign without touching code.

source.section: Build the Atrium That Keeps Humans Sovereign in the Agentic Era
source.passage: **The Primacy Cockpit: Build the Atrium That Keeps Humans Sovereign in the Agentic Era**

chapters.title: The cross-role veto that protects the deal
